cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out the answer to this question. The question is asking why Americans don't take all their vacation days. The options are A to D. First, I'll read through the passage again to make sure I understand the context. The main points seem to be that more than half of Americans don't take all their vacation days, even though they feel they need more holidays. Some who do take breaks still check emails frequently. The expert, Samuel Nahmias, mentions the economic situation, high unemployment rates (9.6%), leading those with jobs to be unwilling to take time off. He also notes that people are more selective about where they go on vacation. In Europe, they have more vacation time, but in the US, many have 6-10 days or less. Workers cite too many responsibilities or stress, or not having time to plan. Technology allows them to stay connected, so they might feel the need to check in.
